name: Legal's Trap
description: Sacrifices the queen to lure the opponent into a losing position, then delivers checkmate with minor pieces. Works when the opponent greedily captures the queen without noticing the forced mate.

name: Noah's Ark Trap
description: Traps White's bishop on the queenside with a pawn avalanche. Black plays a5, b5, and c4 to strand the Bc4/Bb3 bishop with no escape squares.

name: Fried Liver Attack
description: White sacrifices the knight on f7 to expose the Black king and launch a devastating attack. Black's king is forced into the centre and cannot escape the onslaught.

name: Blackburne Shilling Gambit
description: Black offers the e5 pawn to lure White's knight to d4, then traps it with Qg5. White often falls for the fork on f2, losing material.

name: Lasker Trap
description: Black sacrifices a pawn early to gain a powerful pawn on e3, then under-promotes to a knight on d1 to fork the White queen and rook — one of the most surprising traps in chess.

name: Siberian Trap
description: Black allows White to develop aggressively, then springs Ng4 — threatening both the queen and a fork on f2 — winning material when White is not careful.

name: Magnus Smith Trap
description: White sacrifices the e5 pawn to open lines against the Black king after g6, exploiting the weakened dark squares and underdeveloped queenside.

name: Marshall Trap
description: Black lures the White rook to e1 and sacrifices the bishop on h2 to expose the White king, winning material with a discovered check.

name: Fishing Pole Trap
description: Black sets up a hidden trap with Ng4 and h5 in the Berlin Defence structure, dangling the rook as bait. If White accepts, Black delivers a stunning mating attack.

name: Stafford Gambit Trap
description: Black sacrifices a pawn on move 2 for rapid development and dangerous attacking chances. White's natural replies lead into a minefield of tactical tricks threatening both Nxf2 and Ng4.

name: Tennison Gambit: Brigg's Trap
description: White sacrifices the e4 pawn and uses Ng5 and Nc3 to build pressure, luring Black's queen to an overextended square where it becomes a target.

name: Monticelli Trap
description: Black's knight on e4 is tactically vulnerable after Ng5 — White wins the exchange by force. A subtle positional trap in a quiet-looking Indian structure.

name: Elephant Trap
description: Black feigns a normal Queen's Gambit Declined setup, then springs Nd5 followed by Nb4, winning White's queen with a fork if White carelessly plays Nxe4.

name: Albin Countergambit: Lasker Trap
description: Black's rogue pawn on e3 cannot be easily handled. If White plays fxe3, Black promotes to a knight on g1 with check, forking the queen. One of the most shocking underpromotions in opening theory.

name: Beginner's Trap
description: White plays the unorthodox 1.h4 and immediately deploys the rook to h3. Inexperienced opponents who accept the rook offer fall into a forced loss.

name: Budapest Gambit Trap
description: Black sacrifices a pawn to rapidly develop and attack. White's natural moves lead into a pin on e4 where the knight cannot hold, winning material for Black.

name: Englund Gambit Trap
description: Black gambits a pawn immediately to unbalance the game. The resulting positions are full of tricks where White's natural development runs into forks and pins.

name: Petroff Trap
description: White deviates from the main lines and sets up a queen-and-knight coordination that traps Black's queen if Black carelessly recaptures the pawn.

name: Caro-Kann Trap
description: White sets up a kingside pawn storm and Ng5 intrusion after the early piece exchange. Black's natural defensive moves walk into a queen trap or material loss.

name: Sicilian Dragon: Yugoslav Attack Trap
description: White's Yugoslav Attack sacrifices pawns and pieces for a direct kingside assault. Black's natural defensive ideas often fail to the Bh6 exchange followed by a pawn roller.

name: Ruy Lopez: Noah's Ark Trap (Classic)
description: Black traps the White bishop with a queenside pawn advance. After a4-b5-c4 the bishop on b3 (or a4) has no escape and is lost for just a pawn.
